creator: ashish@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Justin Croonenberghs from comment #0)

Sorry for the delay in follow-up.

From the following excerpt from the attachment #170276:

configure:14307: checking for EGifPutExtensionLast in -lgif
configure:14332: cc -o conftest -I/usr/local/include -O2 -pipe -march=native  -isystem /usr/local/include -fstack-protector -fno-strict-aliasing -I/usr/local/include/librsvg-2.0 -I/usr/local/include/glib-2.0 -I/usr/local/lib/glib-2.0/include -I/usr/local/include -I/usr/local/include/gdk-pixbuf-2.0 -pthread -I/usr/local/include/libpng16 -I/usr/local/include/cairo -I/usr/local/include/pixman-1 -I/usr/local/include/freetype2  -I/usr/local/include/libdrm -D_THREAD_SAFE   -I/usr/local/include/ImageMagick-6 -fopenmp -DMAGICKCORE_HDRI_ENABLE=1 -DMAGICKCORE_QUANTUM_DEPTH=16   -I/usr/local/include   -isystem /usr/local/include   -L/usr/local/lib -Wl,-rpath=/usr/lib:/usr/local/lib -fstack-protector -L/usr/local/lib conftest.c -lgif  -L/usr/local/lib -lMagickWand-6 -lMagickCore-6   -L/usr/local/lib -lrsvg-2 -lm -lgio-2.0 -lgdk_pixbuf-2.0 -lgobject-2.0 -lglib-2.0 -lintl -lcairo -lpthread   -lX11 -lpthread -lutil  -L/usr/local/lib -ldbus-1   >&5 
/tmp/conftest-d5ecb2.o: In function `main':
conftest.c:(.text+0x7): undefined reference to `EGifPutExtensionLast'
/usr/local/lib/libMagickCore-6.so: undefined reference to `omp_unset_lock@OMP_3.0'
/usr/local/lib/libMagickCore-6.so: undefined reference to `omp_init_lock@OMP_3.0'
/usr/local/lib/libMagickCore-6.so: undefined reference to `omp_set_lock@OMP_3.0'
/usr/local/lib/libMagickCore-6.so: undefined reference to `omp_destroy_lock@OMP_3.0'
cc: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
configure:14332: $? = 1


It seems like reproduction of bug #191193, where ImageMagick is built with OPENMP option. The problem is nothing specific to Emacs, but will happen with all ports dependent on ImageMagick. Possible workarounds are:

 - Re-install ImageMagick without OPENMP option
 - Build editors/emacs port with GCC compiler

Could you please see if one of these work for you ?

Thanks!
